ety: fr. mustaʻidd
def: s.f. Readiness, promptitude, alertness; fitness, aptness, worthiness, &c.

ety: act. part. of استيمن 'to consider as fortunate; to be happy or blest,' x of يمن 'to be happy, or blest'

def: part. Heard, given ear to, accepted; answered; — acceptable, agreeable

more: (part. act. X. of عجل Heb. and Aram. ) adj. Hastening, alert.

subs: (-ko), To let on hire, to let in farm, to lease.

def: s.m. A species of fragrant grass, Cyperus rotundus; — a particular vegetable poison.
wety: Borrowed from Sanskrit मस्तक (mastaka).

ety: pass. part. of استقبل; see the preceding

wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian مُسْتَقْبِل (mustaqbil), from Arabic مُسْتَقْبَل (mustaqbal).

ety: act. part. of استقبل 'to go forth to meet, to advance towards,' &c., x of قبل 'to come, approach,' &c.

(part. act. X. of قبل Heb. and Aram. ) s. m. Future, the future tense.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian مُسْتَقْبِل (mustaqbil), from Arabic مُسْتَقْبَل (mustaqbal).

ety: pass. part. of استخلص 'to appropriate purely or exclusively to oneself,' x of خلص 'to be or become pure,' &c.

subs: (act. part. of the same verb), part. adj. Extracting, drawing out.

ety: act. part. of استقلّ 'to lift or raise up; to be possessed of absolute power or dominion,' &c., x of قلّ 'to lift; — to be rare,' &c.
def: adj. Peculiar, precise, special; — absolute, despotic, independent; — competent; — stable, firm, durable, permanent; fixed, unmovable; constant, resolute
more: adj. 1. Fixed; immovable. 2. Stable; firm. 3. Constant; resolute. 4. Permanent. mustaqil irādah , n. m. A. firm resolution. mustaqil āsāmī , n. f. A permanent post. mustaqil rahnā , v. n. To be firm; to abide by; to insist. mustaqil mizāj , adj. Firm; resolute; mensequa in arduis .
(part. act. X. of قلّ Heb. and Aram. ) adj. 1. Stable, firm, durable. 2. Absolute, independent, despotick.
subs: (-meṅ), To remain firm (in), to abide (by); to insist; to hold (to)
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian مُسْتَقِل (mustaqil), from Arabic مُسْتَقِلّ (mustaqill).

ety: act. part. of استقام, x of (and syn. with
def: adj. Standing erect; erect, bolt upright; right, straight, direct; — acting uprightly; faithful, true, loyal; — resolute; — well or rightly constituted; having a good tendency
more: adj. قوم stood. Erect; straight. Khat-i-mustaqīm , A straight line.
(part. act. X. of قوم Heb. and Aram. ) adj. Right, straight, standing erect, erect, faithful, loyal, resolute, rightly constituted.
subs: adj. & s.m. Right-sided, rectilineal; — a rectilineal figure.

ety: for A. مستقيمة mustaqīmat, fem. of mustaqīm

ety: for A. مستقيمة mustaqīmat, fem. of mustaqīm
def: adj. = mustaqīm, q.v.
subs: adj. & s.m. Consisting of two right lines; rectilineal.

def: ( Geom .) A rectilineal figure.

ety: act. part. of استقرّ x of (and syn. with

more: (part. act. X. of قرّ Heb. and Aram. ) adj. Remaining firm, constant, firm, stable. ¶ mustaḳarr (part. pass.) s. m. A fixed abode.
subs: (pass. part. of the same verb), s.m. A settled or fixed abode, permanent dwelling-place.
wety: Borrowed from Arabic مُسْتَقَرّ (mustaqarr).

ety: pass. part. of اصطلح 'to agree together respecting a thing,' viii of صلح 'to be good, or right, or correct,' &c.
def: adj. Conventional, technical; idiomatic, phraseological; metaphoric; — s.m. A conventional or technical term; a phrase, or idiom (pl. muṣt̤alaḥāt).
more: (part. pass. VIII. of صلح) s. A technical term, a phrase. Plur. مصطلحات muṣt̤alaḥāt, Phrases, technical terms.

ety: pass. part. of استعمل 'to employ, to make use of,' &c., x of عمل 'to work, to serve,' &c.
def: part. adj. Employed (in work, labour, or service); used, applied; in use; usual, practised; common, established; accustomed; current, not obsolete (a word); — second-hand
more: adj. عمل did. 1. Established; in use; common (جاري 3, and chaltā , 4, 5). 2. Used up; second-hand. mustāmal h. , v. n. To prevail; to be current or in force. gair-mustāmal , adj. Not in use; old-fashioned; obsolete.
(part. pass. X. of عمل Heb. and Aram. ) adj. 1. Used, applied, accustomed, practised, usual. 2. Second-hand.
subs: adj. Unused; not in use, obsolete; old-fashioned.

ety: pass. part. of استمال, x of (and syn. with
def: part. adj. Conciliated; soothed; persuaded.
more: (part. pass. X. of ميل) adj. Conciliated, soothed, persuaded.

def: part. adj. & s.m. Hearing, listening (to); — a hearer, listener.
more: (part. act. VIII. of سمع Heb. and Aram. ) adj. Hearing, a listener.

def: (act. part. cf استأمن 'to demand security, or protection, or safeguard,' &c., x of أمن 'to be or become safe or secure,' &c.), One who implores protection, and takes refuge.

ety: act. part. of استمرّ 'to pass; to be firm,' &c., x of مرّ 'to pass,' &c.

more: (part. act. X. of مرّ) adj. Firm, constant, durable, persevering, passing.

more: (part. pass. X. of مسک Heb. and Aram. ) adj. Bound (to perform something).

(part. pass. VIII. of سند) adj. Supported. ¶ mustanid (part. act.) adj. Relying on, leaning on; one who relies.
subs: (act. part. of the same verb), part. adj. & s.m. Relying (on), leaning (upon or against), supporting oneself (upon); — one who relies, &c.

def: adj. & s.m. (dialec.) = mushṭanḍā, q.v. adj. & s.m. Strong-bodied, robust, stalwart, strapping, lusty; — a robust, or stalwart fellow; — a paramour.
wety: Unknown. Possibly مَسْتَان (mastān, “drunkard, rogue, thug”) contaminated by غُنْڈَہ (ġunḍa, “thug”).

def: adj. (dakh.) Strong-bodied, gigantick.

def: part. adj. & s.m. Asking (or craving) for (water to) drink; — dropsical; — one who craves for (water to) drink; — a dropsical person.
more: adj. سقي gave to drink. Dropsical.
(part. act. X. of سقي Heb. and Aram. ) adj. Craving drink; dropsical.

ety: pass. part. of استثني 'to set aside as excluded; to exclude, or except (from

(part. pass. X. of ثني Heb. ) adj. Excellent, extraordinary, praised, laudable, selected, excepted from any common rule.

more: (part. act. X. of وفي) adj. Paying or receiving in full (a debt). s. m. A head clerk, an auditor. مستوفي گري mustaufī-garī, s. f. Clerkship, audit.

def: part. adj. Deserving, meriting, worthy (of); fitting, proper; — s.m. Author, cause; motive

(part. act. X. of وجب) 1. adj. Worthy, fitting, deserving, meriting. 2. s. m. An author, cause.
subs: Requiring to be received or recovered, recoverable; payable

ety: for A. مستولٍ mustauliṉ, act. part. of استولِي 'to have entire power or authority,' x of of ولی 'to be master of,' &c.

more: (part. act. X. of ولي) adj. Overcoming, subduing, taking possession of, surmounting.

def: adj. Equal, equable, uniform; even, level, flat, plane; straight, right, direct; — moderate; temperate.
more: adj. سوي being equal. Level; plane.
(part. act. VIII. of سوي Heb. and Aram. ) adj. Equal, straight, direct.

ety: pass. part. of استزاد 'to desire or demand an increase or addition,' x of زاد (for زيد
def: part. adj. Increased, augmented; additional; more; — s.m. A kind of poem composed of rhyming verses (after the manner of the g̠azal), each hemistich of which is followed by some additional words in prose, which may also rhyme (e.g. Sun Soz! ʻabas̤ dekhke, ḥairān hogā, Khūbān-kā jamāl; Dil zulf-meṅ uljhegā, pareshān hogā, Mat le yěh wabāl: Yěh ćāl burī hai, tujh-se nibhne-kī nahīṅ, Á mān kahā; Kyā haṅstā hai? tū bahut pashemān hogā, Mat dāṅt nikāl. — Saudā. The merit of these poems is supposed to consist in the fact that it is possible to read them with or without the short additional verses, such as Khūbān-kā jamāl, &c. in the above specimen).
more: n. m. A kind of verse of which every line has a shorter one attached to it.

(part. pass. X. of زيد Heb. ) 1. adj. Increased. 2. s. m. The name of a kind of verse, in which every line has a shorter one annexed to it (as the following from Mīr Soz:-- سن سوزعبث ديکهکي حيران هوگا خوبان کا جمال دل زلف مين الجهيگا پريشان هوگا مت لي يہہ وبال يہہ چال بري هي تجهہ سي نبهنيکي نہين آمان کہا کياهنستاهي توبہت پشيمان هوگا مت دانت نکال Sun Soz! aﻌbas̤ dekhke, ḥairāṅ hogā, ḵẖūbāṅ kā jamāl; dil zulf meṅ uljhegā, pareshāṅ hogā, mat le yih wabāl. Yih chāl burī hai, tujh se nibhne kī nahīṅ, ā mān kahā; kyā haṅstā hai? tū bahut pashemāṅ hogā, mat dānt nikāl. Hear, Soz! by idly looking on the beauty of the fair thou wilt be involved in perplexity; thy heart will be entangled in their tresses and much distressed; do not encounter this trouble. This is a dangerous course, which thou canst not pursue with safety. Come then, listen to my words. Why laughest thou? Do not shew thy teeth, th…

ety: pass. part. of استظہر 'to seek aid or assistance,' x of ظہر 'to overcome, conquer,' &c.; but formed prob. fr. z̤ahr, 'the back'
def: part. adj. Aided, holpen; supported
subs: (act. part. of the same verb), part. adj. & s.m. Imploring (or one who implores) help.

ety: musukā(nā)+āʼī = Prk. एअव्वअइआ = S. (इ)तव्य+ता+इका
def: s.f. (dialec.) = muskurāhaṭ, q.v. s.f. Smiling, smirking; grinning, sniggering; smile, smirk, grin, simper, snigger

ety: S. मूत्रं
def: s.m. Urine
more: n. m. मूत्र, Pālī, muttam . 1. Urine (پيشاب). 2. Semen. 3. A son. 1. Yāhī paiṅḍe pūt hai, yāhī paiṅḍe mūt, Nām līyā to pūt hai, nahīṅ mūt kā mūt . Dohā The same way comes a son, the same way semen's passed, A good son is a son, or semen merely cast. 2. Shāyad kisī Aṅgrez kā tū mūt hai, laṛke! Har bāt meṅ hoṭoṅ pe tere 'ḍām bagar' hai . Some Englishman's vile sperm you sure must be, With every word 'd—n b—r' you mix free. mūt aṭaknā , v. n. To have strangury. mūt-pātr , n. m. 1. A chamber pot. 2. Pudendum muliebre. mūt se nikal-kar gū meṅ girnā , v. n. To escape from the frying-pan to fall into the fire. mūt kā chullū hāth meṅ denā , v. a. To return evil for good. mūt nikalnā , v. n. To piss through fear.
Sant. n. f. 1. A bundle ( gaṭhṛī ). 2. See charas. moṭ'iyā , n. m. A porter; cooly.
subs: (-kā), To labour under a total suppression of urine, to have the strangury
To fall from the frying-pan into the fire
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian موت (mawt), from Arabic مَوْت (mawt).

def: s.m. corr. of mūt, q.v.
wety: Inherited from Prakrit 𑀫𑀁𑀢 (maṃta)), an extension of Ashokan Prakrit 𑀫𑀸 (mā), from Sanskrit मा (mā́), from Proto-Indo-Iranian *máH, from Proto-Indo-European *méh₁.

more: (part. pass. IV. of طوع) adj. 1. Obedient. 2. Obeyed.

s. m. 1. Taking a wife for a time, a temporary marriage of the شيعه Shīʼaﻌs. 2. Use, profit.

more: s. plur. (of متاخر part. act. V. of اخر Heb. ) Modern, late; the moderns.

more: (part. act. V. from عبد Heb. ) adj. 1. Devout, religious. 2. Refractory, stubborn (a camel).

ety: pass. part. of تأدّب 'to learn discipline of the mind and manuers,' v of أدب 'to discipline, educate,' &c.
def: part. adj. Well-disciplined, well-educated, well-bred, polite; learned.
(part. pass. V. of ادب) adj. Wellbred, polite, learned.

ety: pass. part. of تعدّم, v. of (and syn. with

def: adj. & s.m. Who or what passes by or from one to another; (in Gram.) transitive; causal (a verb); — epidemic; contagious (disease); — a transgressor; a wicked wretch, scoundrel.

(part. act. V. from عدو Heb. Chald. ) s. m. 1. A transgressor, a wicked wretch. 2. An active, transitive, or causal (verb).

def: adj. Putrid, rotten, corrupted; stinking, fetid.
more: (part. act. V. from عفن) adj. Putrid, corrupted, rotten, stinking, fetid.

s.m. pl. (obl. pl. in A.) of mutaʻāhid, q.v.

def: part. adj. Appointed; assigned; constituted; deputed; fixed, determined; certain; — s.m. A deputy, locum-tenens; an agent

(part. act. V. from عين Heb. and Chald. ) adj. Appointed, fixed, constituted, deputed, determined; s. m. Deputy, agent. متعين کرنا mutaʼaﻌiyin karna, To appoint, depute, &c.

ety: act. part. of تعجّب, v of (and syn. with
def: part. adj. Full of admiration (for, -se), wondering (at); astonished, amazed; — wonderful, strange

Hanging (to), suspended (to), depending (from); — belonging (to); depending (upon), dependent (upon); connected (with), attached (to); annexed, appended, appendant; subjoined; sticking close (to); occupied or engaged (in); — appertaining (to), touching, concerning, relative (to); pertinent, relevant, applicable; — addicted (to); dedicated (to); — a kinsman; dependant; — an appendage

Pertinent; relevant; applicable. mutaalliq-i-zāt-i-khās , adj. Personal; private. mutaalliq-i-fel ; S. क्रियाविशेषण ( Gram .) Adverb. mutaalliq k. , v. a. 1. To append; join. 2. To extend to; apply; make applicable. 3. To attribute; ascribe; impute. 4. To assign; make over to; to entrust; invest. mutaalliq moharrir yā mutasaddī .
